factors.name: Hot CPI Print
factors.summary: US May CPI +4.2% YoY vs +4.2% expected; headline accelerated from 3.8% prior. Persistent price pressure, especially energy-driven, removes near-term Fed cut expectations and extends 'higher-for-longer' real yields regime, compressing long-duration growth multiples critical to Nasdaq 100 mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Hot inflation data alongside strong USD (DXY >100) implies real yields moving higher, directly bearish for duration-heavy tech stocks that dominate NQ. Long-term growth equities extremely sensitive to 10Y real yield expansion.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation & Strait of Hormuz Closure
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran, Iran's closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, and targeting of naval/air defense assets represent material supply-chain and inflation risks. Oil spiked on supply disruption fears; stagflation concerns rising. Historical playbook: geopolitical shocks + energy supply cuts weigh on high-beta growth equities and tech sentiment.

factors.name: Oracle Heavy Capex Guidance & Debt Raise
factors.summary: Oracle earnings beat but stock slid on plans to raise $40B debt+equity for AI/datacenter buildout and negative free cash flow. Signals hyperscaler AI capex cycle intensifying while balance sheets deteriorate; investors pivoting away from 'growth at any cost' narrative on rising rates.

factors.name: Semis & Mega-Cap Tech Correction Phase
factors.summary: MarketWatch reported semis (Micron, Intel) dragging tech sector into new bearish phase; pullback has 'graduated' to official correction. Validates broader NDX downtrend and suggests institutional rotation out of richly-valued growth into value/defensive.

factors.name: 24h Down -2.17% and Intraday Down -0.48%
factors.summary: Nasdaq 100 already down 2.17% in 24h indicates momentum is in downward phase; move is already extended, suggesting late-stage of selloff rather than early entry point. Participants re-pricing inflation and geopolitical risk.

factors.name: Gold, Commodities, Safe-Haven Bid
factors.summary: Gold broke March lows to $4,050, contradicting typical flight-to-safety narrative on geopolitical escalation (usually gold rallies). Suggests inflation/real-yield concern overriding geopolitical premium. Oil spiked +2% on Hormuz closure but equities fell harder, indicating risk-off dominance.

factors.name: Upcoming Data & Events
factors.summary: Light Asian economic calendar June 11; major catalysts (FOMC, next CPI, NFP) still days away. Markets pricing in higher-for-longer rates; no imminent dovish surprise expected. Fed chair Kevin Warsh reportedly comfortable with elevated inflation target (Trump stated +4.2% acceptable).

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is grinding lower in Phase 2 of a multi-day correction driven by a confluence of macro headwinds: hot CPI print (+4.2% YoY vs expectations), rising real yields, fresh US-Iran military escalation closing the Strait of Hormuz, and investor disappointment in mega-cap tech earnings (Oracle's heavy capex burden). The semis/growth sector has officially entered correction territory per recent reports. Despite geopolitical risk typically supporting safe havens, gold and long-duration assets are being sold alongside equities, indicating the inflation/real-yield regime shift is the dominant driver. A 2.17% 24h decline and already-extended intraday downside suggests we're in late-stage selloff momentum rather than a fresh entry point for bulls.
catalysts_ahead: Producer Price Index (PPI) — timing TBD, will reinforce or challenge inflation narrative
Fed speakers/Warsh comments — market parsing inflation tolerance
Continued Iran-US developments (escalation or deal headlines) — oil/equity volatility spillover
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA if not yet reported) — guidance on AI capex and demand
10Y Treasury yield move (real yield proxy) — critical for NDX duration compression
FOMC meeting (date TBD, likely next 2-3 weeks) — rate path re-evaluation
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks resume and Hormuz closure is lifted, oil could collapse and risk-off premium would evaporate, allowing tech to stabilize and rally. If inflation data rolls over unexpectedly or Fed signals dovish pivot, real yields compress and Nasdaq multiples re-expand. Trump's stated comfort with 4% inflation could signal policy tolerance for lower rates than priced; this would be BULLISH for NDX. Conversely, if geopolitical escalation spreads beyond Iran or energy prices spike further, stagflation fears intensify and growth equities face additional headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended after -2.17% 24h decline. Wait for stabilization and intraday retest of near-term support (~28200–28000) before reconsidering shorts; alternatively, watch for reversal patterns and upticks in VXN as potential bull-trap signals. Do not chase shorts into gap opens lower.
